conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'll be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have a child - my son Alex, he's 7 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few more questions.

Are you currently living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not actually. I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. This is an important factor for Child Benefit eligibility.

Could you tell me which country you're currently living in, and do you have any connection to the UK such as being employed by a UK employer, being subject to UK National Insurance contributions, or having any other official connection to the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm living abroad, but I don't have any UK employment or National Insurance contributions at the moment. I don't have any official connection to the UK right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have one more important question:

Is Alex living with you abroad, or is he living in the UK?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm afraid you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

Here's why:

While Alex is the right age for Child Benefit (children under 16 qualify, or under 20 if in approved education or training), one of the key requirements for Child Benefit is that **you must live in the UK**. 

Since you're currently living abroad with Alex and you don't have any qualifying UK connection (such as UK employment or National Insurance contributions), you don't meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit.

There are limited exceptions for people living abroad who maintain certain UK connections, but these don't apply in your situation as you've confirmed you have no current UK employment, National Insurance contributions, or other official UK ties.

If your circumstances change and you return to live in the UK, you would then be able to apply for Child Benefit at that time.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: According to UK Child Benefit eligibility rules, to qualify for Child Benefit you must be responsible for a child under 16 (or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training) AND you must live in the UK. While the claimant is responsible for Alex who is 7 years old (meeting the age requirement), they are currently living abroad with no connection to the UK through employment, National Insurance contributions, or any other official UK ties. Alex is also living abroad with them. Since neither the claimant nor Alex are living in the UK, and the claimant has no qualifying UK connection, they do not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit eligibility.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for a 7-year-old son, Alex, who meets the age criteria for Child Benefit. However, both the claimant and Alex are living abroad, and the claimant has no current UK employment, National Insurance contributions, or other official connection to the UK. Under UK Child Benefit rules, you must live in the UK to be eligible (with limited exceptions for those with UK employment or NI connections abroad). Since the residency requirement is not met, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
